steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the isosceles condition
steps.content: An isosceles triangle has at least two equal angles. Since angle $R$ is $29^\circ$, one sufficient condition is another angle also measuring $29^\circ$.

steps.title: Test the matching angle
steps.content: The measure of angle S is 29 degrees. says angle $S$ is $29^\circ$. Then angles $R$ and $S$ are equal, so triangle $RST$ must be isosceles.

steps.title: Confirm the other choices
steps.content: The other listed values for angle $S$ do not create a required equal pair with angle $R$. The measure of angle S is 29 degrees. is correct.
